title: Queue state requires reconciliation between the canonical snapshot and append-only projections

observed: operations/ACTIVE_QUEUE.json is timestamped 2026-07-11T16:51:00-04:00 and contains only M-001 for continuity_mining
operations/queue-updates/M-002-2026-07-13T0714-0400.json records a later completed continuity item without rewriting the canonical queue
recent repository history contains additional continuity outputs after the canonical queue timestamp
inferred: operations/ACTIVE_QUEUE.json functions as a lagging canonical snapshot while operations/queue-updates/*.json functions as an append-only projection stream
selecting work from the canonical snapshot alone can duplicate completed work or miss newer unresolved handoffs
proposed: Before selecting a continuity item, merge the canonical snapshot with all later owner-matching queue projections ordered by completed_at or commit time
When snapshot and projection disagree, preserve both observations and mark the effective state as reconciled_from_projection until the canonical queue is intentionally refreshed
Do not overwrite operations/ACTIVE_QUEUE.json from an autonomous team run unless an explicit shared-state reconciliation rule authorizes it
superseded: Treating operations/ACTIVE_QUEUE.json as a complete current view when later queue projection records exist
unresolved: No committed reducer currently materializes an effective queue view from the canonical snapshot plus append-only projections
The repository does not yet declare who may refresh operations/ACTIVE_QUEUE.json or how concurrent projections are resolved

contradiction_resolution.apparent_conflict: The canonical queue shows continuity work ending at M-001, while later projection records show M-002 completed.
contradiction_resolution.resolution: These are not mutually exclusive historical claims. The canonical file is an older snapshot; the projection is a later append-only state transition. Effective current state must be derived from both.
contradiction_resolution.forbidden_conclusion: Do not infer that M-002 is absent, invalid, or still pending merely because it is not present in the older canonical snapshot.

allowed: Public repository paths, blob SHAs, commit SHAs, timestamps, abstract state semantics

falsification_or_review_route: Fetch the cited exact blob and commits. This record is falsified if ACTIVE_QUEUE.json already contains M-002 at the cited blob, if the M-002 projection predates the snapshot, or if a committed repository rule establishes that projections are non-authoritative and must be ignored.
